subject: I think I have the answer.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by JD Vance For Senate Inc
body:
Here's the problem. It's simple.

| [](http://links.jdforohio.org/u/click?_t=52e88da6462b43fd9dbbfea97c7983e9&_m=c2aa4d1f59a54f6ea067ec3547302032&_e=ZZ-7ZUcgWv-rhVMyO6tQ8EtlguKLZoQuB5WJAdE9Mv6sv97T9eR8s99Y8b0-KuFzLLPYAtmo7JFJogLSVZmJnhxkozyXY6yFkontZfWA4vPlM1MvgGpbZkD3FbrYViotBz_MfLzsZRe4nWZQ4QrUE7aVTvoELTC_5yZW00sIwBNI9Rr9BtKHl-USvSbsUOor0_w4H7cOuxyd5NSxRyd5G7jUHGjW3H34eHSwJyoZ53SHTpk-fWleiqOMsi3MvVy94t0ZWZisKO2B1F6VDlcfyR308HKXw_OQsjmg0VdQUXBKzj0dssmmWg28zeV8eoSq3Qi4tpHlNYduzE99GlfWFXOi7TU1TpLGZk5Mt5vARBuvDD7SJI9nodIRaehMicKp8FPAEDUUxv7Cp94ii8OX0tPhhJrej52_QLg46096z7kAzplfDpvntAhMlgpTv_hrV5KRzoWsd39zW6rpj1qe5XWgHpRWARc1qLJUs_Z58iXbR7kdAbS-Ve7LLH7WJMGe1N4CWuUagPH7lv1e40M8Pw%3D%3D)
---
Peter,

America right now is facing more problems than I care to list, but I'll name just a few:

● Broken Southern Border that's allowing fentanyl to flow into our country and destroy American families

● Communist China spying on us and doing everything in their power to cripple our nation and dethrone us as a world power

● Woke policies that our poisoning our youth and threatening the very values that act as the foundation for our nation

Peter, I'm not trying to be pessimistic, but I know you don't want me to sugarcoat anything, either. **I 'm reaching out to you today because there's no way in hell we're going to save our nation from the _**WOKE MOB**_ unless we work together. **

name: LCV Membership Team
subject: (Open this today): Campaign ACTIVE to stop massive Willow oil drilling project. It's not too late to help.
body:
__The Willow project would be the largest oil extraction project on federal land |
---

| Peter -- last week we reached out with an important update that the Biden-Harris administration released the final environmental impact statement on ConocoPhillips' $8 billion Willow oil drilling project in the Arctic.

Willow would lock us into decades of fossil fuel production. This would have devastating environmental impacts, cause health risks to Indigenous people who live nearby, and accelerate climate change at a time when we need to be rapidly shifting _off_ of oil. But it's not too late to reject this project.

**Since we reached out, LCV members from all over the country have pitched in to our campaign to tell the Biden-Harris administration to reverse course.** Many are donating for the first time -- and many are starting monthly gifts to keep our campaign going until we win.

[I'm reaching out today because we haven't heard back from you yet, Peter -- and this is an important one. Can you pitch in before midnight? Just click here -- even $5 is enough to make a difference >>](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/VXb6s8D2ywF~)

As always -- thanks for your support, Peter. See our message from last week for more details -- the stakes couldn't be higher.
-- LCV Membership Team |

* * *

---

|
DEVELOPING... the Biden administration recently issued a final environmental analysis that would allow ConocoPhillips' $8 billion Willow drilling proposal to move forward._****_

If approved, **it would be the largest oil extraction project on federal land.** Our country would be **locking in three more decades of fossil fuel production** at a time when we need to aggressively reduce emissions.____

But Peter -- there's still a chance to reverse course. **We need your help.**

The Biden-Harris administration can still reject the Willow project. We're ramping up a major effort to pressure them to reject this dirty and dangerous project. _The catch is that we need to move quickly._

Right now, we're turning to our members to help fund this pressure campaign over the next few weeks. [Please, help us hit our $20,000 funding goal. If everyone reading this message pitched in -- even $5 -- we'd blow through that goal in minutes.](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/VXb6s8D2ywF~)

The stakes here are massive. The Western Arctic is the cultural homeland for a number of Alaska Native communities, including the Indigenous village of Nuiqsut, which is just 36 miles from the drill site. This fragile area of Alaska is _already_ warming four times faster than the rest of the world due to climate change.

**All of this would add significant additional carbon pollution to our climate -- and irreversibly change the Western Arctic ecosystem.**

We can't let this happen without a fight, Peter. [Please, pitch in today -- even $5 helps. Your gift will fund our hard-hitting environmental campaigns and protect progress for our climate.](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/VXb6s8D2ywF~)

Thanks for all that you do, Peter. We'll be in touch again soon.

Onward,

LCV Membership Team |

---

| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=9221590&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MTY4MDU1MzU4OQS2&spReportId=MTY4MDU1MzU4OQS2). [Click here to unsubscribe](https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=9221590&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MTY4MDU1MzU4OQS2&spReportId=MTY4MDU1MzU4OQS2) from our supporter list. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](mailto:feedback@lcv.org), or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

[740 15th St NW, 7th Floor
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/~) |

[**If you’re with me, please split a donation between Martin Heinrich and my campaign right now to become a Founding Donor. If I’m going to win this thing, I’ll need you by my side from the start.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59114547/393254456/-176078171?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20230222_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZTYxZmFhYy0xMmIzLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eQuNE8PskL2aYErdhOSW-9m41y5kmOZvg4gCL13WFLo=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will immediately be split between Jon Tester and Martin Heinrich:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59114548/393254457/-176078171?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20230222_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZTYxZmFhYy0xMmIzLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eQuNE8PskL2aYErdhOSW-9m41y5kmOZvg4gCL13WFLo=)

[OTHER](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59114553/393254462/-176078171?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20230222_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZTYxZmFhYy0xMmIzLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eQuNE8PskL2aYErdhOSW-9m41y5kmOZvg4gCL13WFLo=)
---
I’m not like most of my colleagues, and it’s not too hard to spot the differences. I’m the only working farmer in the U.S. Senate, and damn proud of it. My wife Sharla and I still work the very same land my grandparents farmed more than 100 years ago.
They didn’t know the meaning of the word “quit.” And neither do I. Our work in the Senate is not done. That’s why I’m running.
It won’t be easy. Mitch McConnell and his allies see me as a top target in their fight to take back the Senate — they’re already running ads attacking me. I’m the most vulnerable Senator running for reelection right now, and experts are rating my race as a toss-up. But I know how to win tough fights. **I’ve done it before as one of the only Democrats in a red state to win in 2018 — and I’ll do it again.**
[**I can’t do it without your support, team. Can I count on you to step up and become a Founding Donor of my reelection campaign today so I can get back to work fighting for regular folks like you and me? You can split a donation between Martin Heinrich and my campaign here >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59114554/393254463/-176078171?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20230222_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZTYxZmFhYy0xMmIzLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eQuNE8PskL2aYErdhOSW-9m41y5kmOZvg4gCL13WFLo=)
I first ran for office because I didn’t see anyone sticking up for dirt farmers like me. In the years since, I’ve demanded that Washington work for the people, fought to get veterans the health care they’ve earned, and ensured Montanans have someone defending our values in the United States Senate.
**And that’s exactly what I’ll continue to do when I’m reelected.**
It’s been the honor of my life to represent Montana families in the United States Senate. I’m fired up and excited for the road ahead, and I’m grateful to have you by my side through it all.
Thanks,
Jon
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59114555/393254464/-176078171?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20230222_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZTYxZmFhYy0xMmIzLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eQuNE8PskL2aYErdhOSW-9m41y5kmOZvg4gCL13WFLo=)
| Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate

| | **WE TOLD YOU:** Democrat Ruben Gallego SURGED ahead of traitor Kyrsten Sinema and MAGA Republican Kari Lake in a brand new Arizona Senate poll.

**AND WE TOLD YOU:** **Flipping this seat blue is the KEY to protecting our fragile one-seat Senate majority, so we can’t let the momentum slip!**

name: Don Huffines
subject: Say it with Me Twice: Expanding the Homestead Exemption Does Not Offer Meaningful Property Tax Relief
body:
New Huffines Liberty Foundation policy paper provides a blueprint forward.
No images? [Click here](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-e-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-p/) [](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-r/) **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE** Press Manager
Contact: [Press@HuffinesLiberty.com](mailto:Press@HuffinesLiberty.com)
## **HLF: Expanding the Homestead Exemption Does Not Offer Meaningful Property Tax Relief**
## _New Huffines Liberty Foundation policy paper provides a blueprint forward._
DALLAS, TX, February 22, 2023 -- It is no secret that the Texas Legislature's use of the homestead exemption to reduce property taxes has provided little actual relief for Texans. Today, the Texas Senate wants to double down on this measure and increase the homestead exemption to $70,000. These savings, we know, will be short-lived, like past increases to the homestead exemption, due to rising appraisal values and tax rates.In a new policy paper from the Huffines Liberty Foundation, we provide a workable blueprint that our friends in Austin--and all concerned Texans--can use to advocate for true property tax relief. [](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-y/) [Read Full Paper](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-j/) Huffines Liberty Foundation President, former State Senator Don Huffines, issued the following statement:_" Increasing the homestead exemption only benefits a select few Texans in a short-lived way. Texans are tired of paying rent to the government and having the value of their businesses destroyed. It's time we realize the unimaginable prosperity for all Texans if we forgo raising the homestead exemption but rather put Texas on the path to eliminate school M&O property taxes, which would benefit all Texas homeowners, businesses, renters, and consumers."_Huffines further added:_" Texas tax problems are largely self-inflicted. That's the bad news. The good news, however, is the fixes needed are uncomplicated. This paper charts the way forward through five easy steps:__1) __Limit state spending growth_
_2) __Freeze school M &O property taxes_
_3) __Use state budget surpluses to eliminate school M &O property taxes _
_4) Require voter approval for local governments to exceed the no-new-revenue tax rate_
_5) Enshrine property tax relief in the Texas Constitution_ _Following this path, the Texas Legislature could cut property taxes almost in half in eight years, or less. "_ [Read Full Paper](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-t/) The Huffines Liberty Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit, non-partisan research institute. Our mission is to advance the cause of **liberty** , **prosperity** , and **virtue** in the State of Texas by educating and equipping citizens so they may hold their elected officials accountable and better informed to tackle the toughest challenges.### [](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-h/)[](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-k/)[](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-u/) [Donate](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-o/) | [](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-b/) | [ Share ](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-fb-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-x/) | [ Tweet ](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-tw-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-f/) | [ Forward ](https://huffineslibertyfoundation.forwardtomyfriend.com/t-yuddtktydi-17329976-fitwhl-l-z)
---|---|---
[www.HuffinesLiberty.com](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-n/)[info@HuffinesLiberty.com](mailto:info@huffinesliberty.com)
(214) 526-3000 The Huffines Liberty Foundation is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization. Contributions are tax-deductible.
By completing forms on this website, you agree to receive marketing and communications from the Huffines Liberty Foundation. [Preferences](https://huffineslibertyfoundation.updatemyprofile.com/t-fitwhl-17329976-yuddtktydi-v) | [Unsubscribe](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-u-fitwhl-yuddtktydi-e/)

Huffines Liberty Foundation President, former State Senator Don Huffines, issued the following statement:" Increasing the homestead exemption only benefits a select few Texans in a short-lived way. Texans are tired of paying rent to the government and having the value of their businesses destroyed. It's time we realize the unimaginable prosperity for all Texans if we forgo raising the homestead exemption but rather put Texas on the path to eliminate school M&O property taxes, which would benefit all Texas homeowners, businesses, renters, and consumers."Huffines further added:" Texas tax problems are largely self-inflicted. That's the bad news. The good news, however, is the fixes needed are uncomplicated. This paper charts the way forward through five easy steps:1) Limit state spending growth 2) Freeze school M &O property taxes 3) Use state budget surpluses to eliminate school M &O property taxes 4) Require voter approval for local governments to exceed the no-new-revenue tax rate 5) Enshrine property tax relief in the Texas Constitution Following this path, the Texas Legislature could cut property taxes almost in half in eight years, or less. " The Huffines Liberty Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit, non-partisan research institute. Our mission is to advance the cause of liberty , prosperity , and virtue in the State of Texas by educating and equipping citizens so they may hold their elected officials accountable and better informed to tackle the toughest challenges.### | | | | ---|---|--- (214) 526-3000 The Huffines Liberty Foundation is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization. Contributions are tax-deductible. By completing forms on this website, you agree to receive marketing and communications from the Huffines Liberty Foundation. |

This Sunday, I was proud to participate in and speak at [a celebration of life in San Francisco](https://substack.com/redirect/58879c61-5880-4f89-8e73-21cccc6e8cb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) organized by Code Pink: Women for Peace, as part of a nationwide "Rage Against the War Machine" day of action. While our gathering in San Francisco featured a variety of politically and ethnically diverse voices, colorful street theater, and energetic performances, its [counterpart](https://substack.com/redirect/315fe3c7-aabb-40b5-b62f-9243becd7d35?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Washington drew [criticism](https://substack.com/redirect/4b747d30-e6f2-427c-a3ab-83348b990946?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from many voices seemingly inclined to place ad hominem critique before the need to rebuild a vibrant and ideologically diverse grassroots movement for peace.
The United States has not pursued a morally defensible foreign policy since the end of the Second World War 75 years ago.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/25edb0ba-ab89-4cac-ad18-346f98b88ecd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo: Liz Duran Boubion
### **More war? Really?**
It is beyond absurd that Congress has [approved the largest military budget in the history of the United States](https://substack.com/redirect/dc0a02ab-932c-454f-96c6-cccf4abbf965?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at a time includes neither a world war, nor the Cold War, nor even the failed regime change experiments of the early 2000s. In the wake of the Pentagon withdrawing from Afghanistan after [20 years of lies](https://substack.com/redirect/fee5c125-2108-465c-88d8-cce221b696ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), it appears that the military industrial complex has simply seized on any excuse for weapon sales, regardless of the cost to national security or international peace.
At the moment, Washington is [actively stoking tension with two nuclear armed powers at once](https://substack.com/redirect/2230508f-8889-45c7-9e11-152ee2cc69d6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as if doing so with only one would be insufficiently idiotic. U.S. officials have publicly hinted at seeking regime change in Russia, prompting its leader to predictably pledge the use of [all of its resources](https://substack.com/redirect/9ccffbdb-9ba8-4eba-a4f7-e88dd09db14e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--including nuclear weapons--to defend itself, before just yesterday [canceling Russia's participation](https://substack.com/redirect/a3f51c95-06b0-415a-b489-fdb9fcff0155?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in a world-historical arms control agreement.
Set aside the fact that there is no particularly compelling geopolitical rationale fueling Biden's escalation with either Russia or China. Even worse is the sheer idiocy of belligerence towards two nuclear-armed powers at once.
More important is the fact that any nuclear conflict would be impossible to contain, either in the sense of limiting fallout to a particular geographic region, or in the sense of preventing an initial conflagration from escalating while expanding to encompass the world in a ball of unsurvivable fire and radiation.
During the Cold War, generations of Americans became sufficiently aware of--and concerned about--the threat of nuclear war that sustained grassroots movements emerged to prevent it. Today, so many have grown inured to American domination of the entire planet that they seem not to recognize the risks of nuclear escalation, or the precarity of a country that can't even get its own citizens medicine contriving international conflict and inviting nuclear escalation on both sides of the globe at once**.**
The absolute senselessness of U.S. foreign policy is a theme that, by now, anyone paying attention has come to sadly recognize. Our country has engaged in [unprovoked invasions](https://substack.com/redirect/044034a6-331e-4372-9780-21477e912f0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) targeting countries from Vietnam to Nicaragua to Iraq; decades of oil wars whose plunder contrived the contemporary climate calamity; and continuing human rights abuses--from [torture with impunity](https://substack.com/redirect/5b80ee06-fb3c-4070-a8d2-baf27df7fd8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to arbitrary drone strikes leaving untold numbers dead for no reason. The United States has not pursued a morally defensible foreign policy since the end of the Second World War 75 years ago.
Today's nuclear brinksmanship is also dangerous due to the economic interdependence established between China, Russia, and the United States. The bombing of the Nordstream pipeline offers a compelling example of how conflict hurts everyone.
Investigative journalist [Seymour M. Hersh](https://substack.com/redirect/622dbd52-2b20-4700-b209-00251292b60f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) once exposed a [massacre](https://substack.com/redirect/5c991c86-764a-4a23-8a47-9d2dca90cc0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) committed by U.S. Marines who murdered hundreds of unarmed civilians in Mai Lai, Vietnam, while raping and mutilating dozens (if not hundreds) of women. That American crime against humanity was suppressed by the Pentagon for a year, before later being spun for the public by a compliant young military officer named Colin Powell, who would go on to lie our country into another war 30 years later.
More recently, Hersh [exposed](https://substack.com/redirect/8e46698b-4d62-47ca-8021-58bce053f78e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the Nordstream pipeline was bombed by the United States, despite its public presentations blaming other countries (which, incidentally, matches [a long pattern](https://substack.com/redirect/2526ba01-f9e7-4816-a0dd-1ecac6637016?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) observable across many decades) and the dramatic harm to the interests of our allies, particularly Germany. Hersh reports:
> Last June...Navy divers, operating under the cover of a widely publicized mid-summer NATO exercise known as [BALTOPS 22](https://substack.com/redirect/856e827c-fa09-46b5-ade8-c9ad08878323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), planted the remotely triggered explosives that, three months later, destroyed three of the four Nord Stream pipelines, according to a source with direct knowledge of the operational planning.
> Two of the pipelines, which were known collectively as Nord Stream 1, had been providing Germany and much of Western Europe with cheap Russian natural gas for more than a decade. A second pair of pipelines, called Nord Stream 2, had been built but were not yet operational. Now, with Russian troops massing on the Ukrainian border and the bloodiest war in Europe since 1945 looming, President Joseph Biden saw the pipelines as a vehicle for Vladimir Putin to weaponize natural gas for his political and territorial ambitions.
Even short of nuclear escalation, the latest round of chest beating has already led to [severe costs](https://substack.com/redirect/dc3b7029-0c4b-434a-9949-e5d4d80d7301?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) borne by civilians not only across Europe and Asia, but also the rest of the world impacted by predictable disruptions in the supply & shipping of grain disregarded by the Pentagon.
Civilians across the United States are feeling related pain, as well. Military conflict in eastern Europe has [driven increasing inflation](https://substack.com/redirect/a9dfe366-93e0-4942-95eb-e09e109adeb4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that continues to erode the savings of workers and especially retirees.
Meanwhile, train derailments across the country suggest--at the very least--that Biden's transportation secretary, Pete Buttigieg, is [out of his league](https://substack.com/redirect/d4f18097-6f69-4cf3-b699-c330432db4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and unable to satisfactorily perform in a federal role. Given his [catastrophic performance](https://substack.com/redirect/76684fe6-830c-49dd-a240-3faa2016a568?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a small town mayor, that should not particularly surprise anyone.
But [some voices](https://substack.com/redirect/02023677-d655-4f85-aaeb-7c75dd2e4582?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) have gone further, suggesting that the train derailments might indicate not only the incompetence of co-opted federal regulators, but also potential cyber attacks spearheaded by the intelligence agencies of foreign countries.
Military whistleblowers have revealed a longstanding preceding war over cyberspace--yet again, [initiated by the United States](https://substack.com/redirect/a0a6b29e-347d-496e-b913-513ffd279ab1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--rendering such speculation terrifyingly plausible. Potential culprits include one (China) that claims [the Pentagon recently shot down its weather balloons](https://substack.com/redirect/0f099a26-5e64-4364-86fc-fb6370d11de2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after reportedly mischaracterizing them, or another (Russia) [blamed](https://substack.com/redirect/34967989-57fa-40f8-b88a-0df6ab3bf90a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for sabotaging its own natural gas pipeline before independent investigative journalists [exposed](https://substack.com/redirect/8e46698b-4d62-47ca-8021-58bce053f78e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the Pentagon's previously secret machinations.
On the other hand, over [800 trains derailed across the United States in 2022](https://substack.com/redirect/8b4ec7ae-5092-4935-ba5c-3cb90abe982b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), suggesting that the pace of derailments might not have changed, at all. If nothing else, this zone of apparent ambiguity might be a good reason for the press to finally begin guarding its vestigial credibility, which continues to [catastrophically decline](https://substack.com/redirect/e7304119-237c-4ff8-a6fc-5731714fa13c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with dangerous consequences for democracy.
The truth, we all know, is the very first casualty of war. And having been at war nearly constantly for the last three generations, America seems to have lost its capacity to discern the difference between fact and fiction. That's unfortunately not surprising given the [abdication of journalism](https://substack.com/redirect/4def5d33-18b7-4a03-b558-2d6e75331995?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by editors who instead act as cheerleaders for Wall Street's imperial whims.
Given the role of complicit journalists in brainwashing the public and fanning the flames for international conflict, I am deeply grateful for you taking the time to read a suppressed perspective! Please share it with your friends to help ensure that, if America does take further steps towards destroying yet more life on Earth, at least we might do it with our eyes open for once.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=104522829&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTA0NTIyODI5LCJpYXQiOjE2NzcxMTcyNDksImV4cCI6MTY3OTcwOTI0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4ki67othvQe_cO6IhkR6zgsjbv98I-JRDHzFWHVBtS8)
### **Divide and Conquer**
Since the era of colonialism, military industrial powers have [relied](https://substack.com/redirect/49092fed-54a8-4f36-9fb2-3cc2e7402335?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on the tactic of dividing opponents in order to conquer them. That tactic enabled the transatlantic slave trade, global European colonialism, and it continues to enable the machinations of the Pentagon and America's military industrial establishment today.
The "Rage against the War Machine" rally in Washington was widely criticized by voices, including many on the left, who took objection to the inclusion among the organizers and speakers of voices who stand on the right. It was striking how many local groups that claim to care about "progressive" principles proved strikingly absent.
Those who criticize politically ecumenical events mistake both the nature of those events, as well as their own position as marginalized dissidents in an empire unthreatened by their critique.
[Many left-leaning voices](https://substack.com/redirect/ed82ec3c-a469-4ed2-8a08-f31b9282d667?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) have argued that marching and speaking alongside libertarian figures, and other conservatives who oppose war, counterproductively enables those figures by "platforming" them.
That perspective, however, is confused. The voices decried by left-leaning observers already have platforms, including a national television network and communities created by filter bubbles across every social media platform. To the extent there is a comparison to be made, the media organs of the left tend to reach much smaller audiences than their counterparts on the right--suggesting that, if anything, it was conservatives running the greater risk by platforming more progressive voices.
Given the need to restrain a military-industrial complex much bigger than either of the ideological wings, "platforming" diverse voices is the least of our worries. Allowing concerns about supposedly legitimating ideologically diverse voices to impede grassroots collaboration only strengthens the war machine. Any effort to prevent nuclear war is worth at least pursuing, rather than abandoning at the outset due to suspicion and mistrust.
In this context, critics arguing against grassroots participation resemble the very same writers whose "journalism" so frequently parrots government disinformation, or perhaps policymakers wearing clever designer gowns to a gala while millions starve in the streets. Dissent without corresponding action is largely meaningless, and ultimately more theatrical than political.
Insisting on ideological purity at a time like this is foolish for other reasons, as well. First, most organizations that claim to stand on the left demonstrated their active [hypocrisy and co-optation](https://substack.com/redirect/82e578b7-65cf-4fc4-aef5-0c1025fc6cee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a long time ago, and have no legitimacy to cast stones from their respective glass houses. Every organization in San Francisco, for instance, that claims to stand for "progressive" values lined up behind an [unrepentantly corrupt insider trading oligarch](https://substack.com/redirect/2dc613ba-cd3a-4e88-a637-0ea5ac917e7d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2020, and continues to support the runaway corruption of the corporate Democratic Party. In other words, whatever passes for "the left" in the U.S. has little integrity to begin with.
Second, looking beyond our cloistered communities, the distribution of political power in the United States today also counsels engaging diverse allies for strategic reasons. I wrote over a decade ago about the [need for the left and right to join forces](https://substack.com/redirect/3eb4c7e4-f7f0-4701-8e48-a56bd9373b7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to challenge the corrupt corporate center, precisely because neither of the ideological wings enjoys sufficient support to dislodge the center on its own. I went on to effectuate the strategy I articulated across the country through [organizing](https://substack.com/redirect/1a9a2250-cbaa-42bf-aab3-ece572b42f2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) projects that [successfully](https://substack.com/redirect/574f6f5a-61bc-4235-8c1c-4e9809bfe96e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) shifted local policing [policies](https://substack.com/redirect/82e29cf0-a6a5-4d7e-85e7-ac35658af13f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [practices](https://substack.com/redirect/199bc9e2-5878-4152-af5e-e2a0ddb10d57?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in several jurisdictions at the local level.
Limiting any coalition to a single ideological perspective is ultimately self-marginalizing. Given the history in the United States over the past several decades, any strategy insisting on ideological purity invites defeat, privileging virtue signaling over peace, and the satisfaction of adherents over the lives of innocent people--and species--placed at foolish risk for nothing.
### **My highlights from Sunday**
I had the honor of addressing the San Francisco gathering, following a series of compelling speakers, including:
* Eric Garris from antiwar.com, who discussed the corruption of the bipartisan war machine;
* Arieann Harrison, founder of the Marie Harrison Foundation in Bayview Hunters Point, who discussed how militarism has particularly harmed Black San Franciscans and led to [a local environmental justice disaster](https://substack.com/redirect/690fcd69-b0c8-4783-9715-f8f31638ed15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg);
* John Walsh from Veterans for Peace, who addressed the costs to military servicemembers and their families;
* Starchild from the Libertarian Party of SF, who argued in favor of defunding the military industrial establishment;
* former CIA analyst Raymond McGovern, who addressed the gathering remotely;
* musician and activist Francisco Herrera, whose songs offered vital energy and passion on a cold (though thankfully sunny) day;
* organizer Cynthia Papermaster from CODEPINK SF Bay Area and Extinction Rebellion Peace, who discussed the background behind the gathering and shared, among other things, delightful introductions to a series of "Dogs Against Nukes" who joined us; and.
* Cheryl Davila, former Berkeley City Council Member and Director of the Climate Emergency Task Force, who spoke outside the Lockheed Martin office about budget priorities and the dire needs confronting our communities from which war is, at best, a distraction.

My comments focused on encouraging ongoing participation despite the controversy surrounding the event, exposing the senselessness of military escalation, and exploring the idiotic complicity of journalists who have abandoned independence and their own professional ethics in the service of imperialism and the business opportunities that come with it.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/87c916f7-0eac-44cb-b280-e7387d8014c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Dancer & choreographer Liz Duran Boubion offered a compelling performance depicting the surreal calamity of nuclear fallout, and a vibrant group of street theater performers gathered outside the San Francisco office of Lockheed Martin to creatively explore the intersections between corporate Democrats, weapons manufacturers, wealthy campaign donors, and the corrupt racket that moves funds between each of those sectors.
No single event offers the promise of ending a challenge so longstanding as militarism. But if Sunday's gathering was any indication, the movement for peace has planted a new seed. I'm eager to see it grow.
Help expand the movement for sanity and peace by sharing this post!

clean_body: Thousands from coast-to-coast took action to challenge militarism, while ideological divisions continue to enable the bipartisan military industrial establishment | | ---|---|--- | | | or ? ? Thousands from coast-to-coast took action to challenge militarism, while ideological divisions continue to enable the bipartisan military industrial establishment | | | Feb 23| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | ? This Sunday, I was proud to participate in and speak at organized by Code Pink: Women for Peace, as part of a nationwide "Rage Against the War Machine" day of action. While our gathering in San Francisco featured a variety of politically and ethnically diverse voices, colorful street theater, and energetic performances, its in Washington drew from many voices seemingly inclined to place ad hominem critique before the need to rebuild a vibrant and ideologically diverse grassroots movement for peace. The United States has not pursued a morally defensible foreign policy since the end of the Second World War 75 years ago. | | ---|---|--- Photo: Liz Duran Boubion More war? Really? It is beyond absurd that Congress has at a time includes neither a world war, nor the Cold War, nor even the failed regime change experiments of the early 2000s. In the wake of the Pentagon withdrawing from Afghanistan after , it appears that the military industrial complex has simply seized on any excuse for weapon sales, regardless of the cost to national security or international peace. At the moment, Washington is , as if doing so with only one would be insufficiently idiotic. U.S. officials have publicly hinted at seeking regime change in Russia, prompting its leader to predictably pledge the use of nuclear weapons--to defend itself, before just yesterday in a world-historical arms control agreement. Set aside the fact that there is no particularly compelling geopolitical rationale fueling Biden's escalation with either Russia or China. Even worse is the sheer idiocy of belligerence towards two nuclear-armed powers at once. More important is the fact that any nuclear conflict would be impossible to contain, either in the sense of limiting fallout to a particular geographic region, or in the sense of preventing an initial conflagration from escalating while expanding to encompass the world in a ball of unsurvivable fire and radiation. During the Cold War, generations of Americans became sufficiently aware of--and concerned about--the threat of nuclear war that sustained grassroots movements emerged to prevent it. Today, so many have grown inured to American domination of the entire planet that they seem not to recognize the risks of nuclear escalation, or the precarity of a country that can't even get its own citizens medicine contriving international conflict and inviting nuclear escalation on both sides of the globe at once. The absolute senselessness of U.S. foreign policy is a theme that, by now, anyone paying attention has come to sadly recognize. Our country has engaged in targeting countries from Vietnam to Nicaragua to Iraq; decades of oil wars whose plunder contrived the contemporary climate calamity; and continuing human rights abuses--from to arbitrary drone strikes leaving untold numbers dead for no reason. The United States has not pursued a morally defensible foreign policy since the end of the Second World War 75 years ago. Today's nuclear brinksmanship is also dangerous due to the economic interdependence established between China, Russia, and the United States. The bombing of the Nordstream pipeline offers a compelling example of how conflict hurts everyone. Investigative journalist once exposed a committed by U.S. Marines who murdered hundreds of unarmed civilians in Mai Lai, Vietnam, while raping and mutilating dozens (if not hundreds) of women. That American crime against humanity was suppressed by the Pentagon for a year, before later being spun for the public by a compliant young military officer named Colin Powell, who would go on to lie our country into another war 30 years later. More recently, Hersh that the Nordstream pipeline was bombed by the United States, despite its public presentations blaming other countries (which, incidentally, matches observable across many decades) and the dramatic harm to the interests of our allies, particularly Germany. Hersh reports: Last June...Navy divers, operating under the cover of a widely publicized mid-summer NATO exercise known as , planted the remotely triggered explosives that, three months later, destroyed three of the four Nord Stream pipelines, according to a source with direct knowledge of the operational planning. Two of the pipelines, which were known collectively as Nord Stream 1, had been providing Germany and much of Western Europe with cheap Russian natural gas for more than a decade. A second pair of pipelines, called Nord Stream 2, had been built but were not yet operational. Now, with Russian troops massing on the Ukrainian border and the bloodiest war in Europe since 1945 looming, President Joseph Biden saw the pipelines as a vehicle for Vladimir Putin to weaponize natural gas for his political and territorial ambitions. Even short of nuclear escalation, the latest round of chest beating has already led to borne by civilians not only across Europe and Asia, but also the rest of the world impacted by predictable disruptions in the supply & shipping of grain disregarded by the Pentagon. Civilians across the United States are feeling related pain, as well. Military conflict in eastern Europe has that continues to erode the savings of workers and especially retirees. Meanwhile, train derailments across the country suggest--at the very least--that Biden's transportation secretary, Pete Buttigieg, is and unable to satisfactorily perform in a federal role. Given his as a small town mayor, that should not particularly surprise anyone. But have gone further, suggesting that the train derailments might indicate not only the incompetence of co-opted federal regulators, but also potential cyber attacks spearheaded by the intelligence agencies of foreign countries. Military whistleblowers have revealed a longstanding preceding war over cyberspace--yet again, such speculation terrifyingly plausible. Potential culprits include one (China) that claims after reportedly mischaracterizing them, or another (Russia) for sabotaging its own natural gas pipeline before independent investigative journalists the Pentagon's previously secret machinations. On the other hand, over , suggesting that the pace of derailments might not have changed, at all. If nothing else, this zone of apparent ambiguity might be a good reason for the press to finally begin guarding its vestigial credibility, which continues to with dangerous consequences for democracy. The truth, we all know, is the very first casualty of war. And having been at war nearly constantly for the last three generations, America seems to have lost its capacity to discern the difference between fact and fiction. That's unfortunately not surprising given the by editors who instead act as cheerleaders for Wall Street's imperial whims. Given the role of complicit journalists in brainwashing the public and fanning the flames for international conflict, I am deeply grateful for you taking the time to read a suppressed perspective! Please share it with your friends to help ensure that, if America does take further steps towards destroying yet more life on Earth, at least we might do it with our eyes open for once. Divide and Conquer Since the era of colonialism, military industrial powers have on the tactic of dividing opponents in order to conquer them. That tactic enabled the transatlantic slave trade, global European colonialism, and it continues to enable the machinations of the Pentagon and America's military industrial establishment today. The "Rage against the War Machine" rally in Washington was widely criticized by voices, including many on the left, who took objection to the inclusion among the organizers and speakers of voices who stand on the right. It was striking how many local groups that claim to care about "progressive" principles proved strikingly absent. Those who criticize politically ecumenical events mistake both the nature of those events, as well as their own position as marginalized dissidents in an empire unthreatened by their critique. have argued that marching and speaking alongside libertarian figures, and other conservatives who oppose war, counterproductively enables those figures by "platforming" them. That perspective, however, is confused. The voices decried by left-leaning observers already have platforms, including a national television network and communities created by filter bubbles across every social media platform. To the extent there is a comparison to be made, the media organs of the left tend to reach much smaller audiences than their counterparts on the right--suggesting that, if anything, it was conservatives running the greater risk by platforming more progressive voices. Given the need to restrain a military-industrial complex much bigger than either of the ideological wings, "platforming" diverse voices is the least of our worries. Allowing concerns about supposedly legitimating ideologically diverse voices to impede grassroots collaboration only strengthens the war machine. Any effort to prevent nuclear war is worth at least pursuing, rather than abandoning at the outset due to suspicion and mistrust. In this context, critics arguing against grassroots participation resemble the very same writers whose "journalism" so frequently parrots government disinformation, or perhaps policymakers wearing clever designer gowns to a gala while millions starve in the streets. Dissent without corresponding action is largely meaningless, and ultimately more theatrical than political. Insisting on ideological purity at a time like this is foolish for other reasons, as well. First, most organizations that claim to stand on the left demonstrated their active a long time ago, and have no legitimacy to cast stones from their respective glass houses. Every organization in San Francisco, for instance, that claims to stand for "progressive" values lined up behind an in 2020, and continues to support the runaway corruption of the corporate Democratic Party. In other words, whatever passes for "the left" in the U.S. has little integrity to begin with. Second, looking beyond our cloistered communities, the distribution of political power in the United States today also counsels engaging diverse allies for strategic reasons. I wrote over a decade ago about the to challenge the corrupt corporate center, precisely because neither of the ideological wings enjoys sufficient support to dislodge the center on its own. I went on to effectuate the strategy I articulated across the country through projects that shifted local policing and in several jurisdictions at the local level. Limiting any coalition to a single ideological perspective is ultimately self-marginalizing. Given the history in the United States over the past several decades, any strategy insisting on ideological purity invites defeat, privileging virtue signaling over peace, and the satisfaction of adherents over the lives of innocent people--and species--placed at foolish risk for nothing. My highlights from Sunday I had the honor of addressing the San Francisco gathering, following a series of compelling speakers, including: Eric Garris from antiwar.com, who discussed the corruption of the bipartisan war machine; Arieann Harrison, founder of the Marie Harrison Foundation in Bayview Hunters Point, who discussed how militarism has particularly harmed Black San Franciscans and led to ; John Walsh from Veterans for Peace, who addressed the costs to military servicemembers and their families; Starchild from the Libertarian Party of SF, who argued in favor of defunding the military industrial establishment; former CIA analyst Raymond McGovern, who addressed the gathering remotely; musician and activist Francisco Herrera, whose songs offered vital energy and passion on a cold (though thankfully sunny) day; organizer Cynthia Papermaster from CODEPINK SF Bay Area and Extinction Rebellion Peace, who discussed the background behind the gathering and shared, among other things, delightful introductions to a series of "Dogs Against Nukes" who joined us; and. Cheryl Davila, former Berkeley City Council Member and Director of the Climate Emergency Task Force, who spoke outside the Lockheed Martin office about budget priorities and the dire needs confronting our communities from which war is, at best, a distraction. My comments focused on encouraging ongoing participation despite the controversy surrounding the event, exposing the senselessness of military escalation, and exploring the idiotic complicity of journalists who have abandoned independence and their own professional ethics in the service of imperialism and the business opportunities that come with it. Dancer & choreographer Liz Duran Boubion offered a compelling performance depicting the surreal calamity of nuclear fallout, and a vibrant group of street theater performers gathered outside the San Francisco office of Lockheed Martin to creatively explore the intersections between corporate Democrats, weapons manufacturers, wealthy campaign donors, and the corrupt racket that moves funds between each of those sectors. No single event offers the promise of ending a challenge so longstanding as militarism. But if Sunday's gathering was any indication, the movement for peace has planted a new seed. I'm eager to see it grow. Help expand the movement for sanity and peace by sharing this post! Paid subscribers can access below a song (on Spotify or Apple Music) that I wrote 20 years ago to draw attention to the senseless of militarism and its devastating impacts on military families. I frankly wish it would have grown less relevant over time. I have long worked to expose corruption and promote creative efforts to challenge it. While a racist character assassination ultimately ended my career in law & politics, I can't stop speaking truth to power and appreciate any support. Your paid subscription helps put me in a position to focus less time on wage work and more on research & writing to inform the public discussion. ... name: Joe Biden
subject: I'm asking, Derek: A​re you with me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, **[www
body:
For as much as we’ve gotten done over the past two years, we have some unfinished business on our agenda. | |
---
I ran for president for three reasons -- to restore the soul of the nation, to rebuild the backbone of the nation, and to unite our country. Over the past two years, the economy has created 12 million new jobs under Kamala and me -- the strongest two years of growth in history by a longshot. We also made the biggest investment in American infrastructure since President Eisenhower's administration -- in highways, bridges, ports, airports, clean water systems, high-speed internet, and rail. All of it. I have to be honest: For as much as we’ve gotten done over the past two years, we have some unfinished business on our agenda. We need to make the cost of insulin $35/month for every American, pass affordable child care and family paid leave, ban assault weapons, codify _Roe v. Wade_ , and so much more. **Kamala and I can’t do it alone -- it’s going to take the full support of this grassroots team to keep pushing our agenda forward. Will you make a $7 donation to the DNC today to invest in the resources Democrats are relying on to win races in every corner of this great nation?**

Republicans keep talking about what they're going to do. So, let's make it real simple: If Republicans try to cut Social Security -- that's not going to get passed by the Senate in my view, but if it does -- I'll stop them. _I got a veto._ And if they send me a national ban on the right to choose, _I will stop them._ But stopping Republicans is just the half of it. It is going to take electing Democrats up and down the ballot to keep building a better America in the next few years. And the Democratic National Committee is at the heart of that work. **So let me ask you a simple question: Are you with us?** **[If you are, I hope you’ll show your support for our party by making a $7 donation to the DNC today. clean_body: For as much as we’ve gotten done over the past two years, we have some unfinished business on our agenda. | | I ran for president for three reasons -- to restore the soul of the nation, to rebuild the backbone of the nation, and to unite our country. Over the past two years, the economy has created 12 million new jobs under Kamala and me -- the strongest two years of growth in history by a longshot. We also made the biggest investment in American infrastructure since President Eisenhower's administration -- in highways, bridges, ports, airports, clean water systems, high-speed internet, and rail. All of it. I have to be honest: For as much as we’ve gotten done over the past two years, we have some unfinished business on our agenda. We need to make the cost of insulin $35/month for every American, pass affordable child care and family paid leave, ban assault weapons, codify Roe v. Wade , and so much more. Kamala and I can’t do it alone -- it’s going to take the full support of this grassroots team to keep pushing our agenda forward. Will you make a $7 donation to the DNC today to invest in the resources Democrats are relying on to win races in every corner of this great nation? Republicans keep talking about what they're going to do. So, let's make it real simple: If Republicans try to cut Social Security -- that's not going to get passed by the Senate in my view, but if it does -- I'll stop them. I got a veto. And if they send me a national ban on the right to choose, I will stop them. But stopping Republicans is just the half of it. It is going to take electing Democrats up and down the ballot to keep building a better America in the next few years. And the Democratic National Committee is at the heart of that work. So let me ask you a simple question: Are you with us? Thank you and God bless you. Joe Joseph R. Biden, Jr. President of the United States Manage how often you receive DNC emails, . If you no longer wish to receive emails from the DNC, **. If you're ready to elect Democrats in all 50 states, territories, and D.C., . Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible. Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, ** , and not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

subject: $5 is all it could take
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party
body:
to support Republican victories.

http://wsrp.org
Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WeGlgXqOx-Y

With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms.

We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! (https://form.jotform.com/212737084845058)
State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates
https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws
State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. (https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws)
Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate
Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press:

“We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions.

“Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative."

Read the full statement. (https://jtwilcox.houserepublicans.wa.gov/2021/10/18/republican-leaders-todays-vaccine-deadline-threatens-critical-services-public-safety/?fbclid=IwAR3WxI9H29hbbmfbZhdkDS9QjqdVd2TIRCdjNjUkUlLdeThtbUjK_WBWJuw)
Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter
Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here (https://www.smileyforwashington.com/issues) .
Contact:
Steve Johnson
Communications Director
Washington State Republican Party
(425) 460-0570
steve@wsrp.org (mailto:steve@wsrp.org)

============================================================
** View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/wsrp/5forvictory?e=6a281f566d)
Copyright © 2023 Washington State Republican Party, All rights reserved.
Your interest in the Washington State Republican Party, National Republican Committee or Washington state Republican Candidates

Our mailing address is:
Washington State Republican Party
11811 NE 1st St Suite 306
Bellevue, WA 98005
USA

You can ** update your preferences (https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/profile?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=4f90cbb438&e=6a281f566d&c=96fc8a26e8)
or ** unsubscribe from this list (https://wsrp.us2.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=be1a8605f1118163852707f35&id=4f90cbb438&e=6a281f566d&c=96fc8a26e8)
.

**admin@alaskansforhonestelections.com or call 907-802-8116****We only need 26,000 signatures, which is the lowest amount in history since we had the lowest turnout by percentage in history. Rank Choice Voting has made our job even easier. Let’s Do This Alaska.****If you would like to help us collect signatures, please sign up here** https://www.alaskansforhonestelections.com/petition-signatures**The Pro Rank Choice Voting group paid $300k and used mostly out of state signature gatherers, we want this done “By Alaskans, For Alaskans”**
---
#### **An Alaska Senator has said that our Bill is the Most Important Bill for the 2024 Election. We need you to get there. Help us Today!!!**
---
---
|
---
### **Come Sign Today**
#### **Want To Sign The First Petition To Get Rid Of Rank Choice Voting!!!**

There are 40 House Districts, we need at least 35 or 75% of the districts. Attached is a breakdown of how many signatures we need per district. We will need signature gatherers in each district. The sign up link is the only way we can keep track of all of the people who are getting petition books. The group that passed Ranked Choice Voting in 2020 spent over $300,000 just on the signature gathering process, they paid an out of state firm to gather signatures here in Alaska. We want to have Alaskans in charge this time around. They collected 40,000 signatures, we want to collect 75,000.
Link to sign up for a Petition Book
[https://www.alaskansforhonestelections.com/petition-signatures](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=63f6f0557e236f729b976bee&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.alaskansforhonestelections.com%2Fpetition-signatures%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D63f6efab0baf441a0cd5a4a7%26ss_email_id%3D63f6f0557e236f729b976bee%26ss_campaign_name%3DKenai%2BSigning%2Bis%2BFeb%2B23rd%252C%2Bnot%2BMarch%2B23rd.%2BFixed%2Bthat.%2BPlease%2Bsee%2Bwebsite%25E2%2580%25A6%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2023-02-23T04%253A50%253A01Z&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_63f6efab0baf441a0cd5a4a7&l=en-US&s=EA-_dO8K5Lwfu5Bwi4VDCvIsFGA%3D)Please use the link below to find your house district or include your address if you do not know your house district. Scroll to the bottom and it has a search by address, which should tell you Your House and Senate Representatives.
[https://akleg.gov/Districts.php](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=63f6f0557e236f729b976bee&u=https%3A%2F%2Fakleg.gov%2FDistricts.php&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_63f6efab0baf441a0cd5a4a7&l=en-US&s=ywwSwJ9D9WI8SDl6SwT3tEbkKxM%3D)**To get involved please email**[**Admin@AlaskansForHonestElections.com**](mailto:admin@alaskansforhonestelections.com?subject=Newsletter%20Contact)

---
### Please Share with Friends, Family, and Co-Workers!
---
**We have completed two parts to the Petition Process, please read 3 and 4 below to learn about the next steps.****1.** Collect 100 Sponsor Signatures**(Completed November 23rd, 2022)****2.** After the Lt. Governor approves our initiative, we will need to collect 10% of the most recent General Election, which is the lowest turnout in 12 years, so our actual signatures needed will be less than 40,000. At this point (December-January 2022) we are collecting sponsor signatures to take a lead roll in removing Ranked Choice Voting from Alaska.**(Completed January 20th, 2023)****3\. We now need to collect signatures from at least 30,000 Registered Alaskan Voters, we will receive 500 Petition Books, each Book contains 150 Signatures. We need help, please see our timeline and either email, call, or text us if you would like to get involved.****4\. We will need signatures from 75% of the House Districts, We plan to collect signatures from 100% of the 40 House Districts. We will need to collect 10% of the total number of voters in the previous election in each House District. Please let us know if you have questions about this.**
---
[_ _ Call Us or Text Us Here _ _](tel:+19078028116)
---
---
|
---
**Rank choice was sold to Alaska by outsiders as a means to control dark money. Outsiders put more of their money than ever before in our elections. It did not work!****99% of their funding came from out of Alaska.****Rank Choice voting has proven to discourage voter turnout. This past election had the lowest turn out since 2010. It is so complicated that 1000’s did not vote.****Some voters, votes are counted 2 or 3 times. Not fair! It should be 1 person 1 vote.****This is not a partisan issue. Rank Choice voting affected everyone. In at least 1 race the Democrat received less that 15% of the vote.****Rank Choice creates division and bitterness.**
---
[_ _ Email Us Today _ _](mailto:info@alaskansforhonestgovernment.org?subject=Newsletter)
---
---

name: Brent Bozell
subject: 10 BIG reveals about Hunter Biden the media refuses to cover
body:
Congress begins its first public hearings on President Joe Biden’s family, including Hunter and his ties to foreign companies.

Congress begins its first public hearings on President Joe Biden’s family, including Hunter and his ties to foreign companies.

[](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zp7s497opgtgkjflmdgkl1hl7lmo1q2qb218tvgo_rp22sh2s8i66pr7chimcor1c1h2bs)
Friend,

Congress begins its first public hearings on President Joe Biden’s family, including Hunter and his ties to foreign companies.

But you won’t find anything about it in the mainstream media.

_**ABC, CBS, and NBC refuse to give these stories the attention they deserve. **_

It’s not just the salacious stories of drug use, illegal gun possession, hookers, and nude photos they ignore. Some cases show Joe Biden lied in 2019: _“I’ve never spoken to my son [Hunter] about his overseas business dealings!”_

Let me introduce myself. My name is Brent Bozell, Founder and President of the Media Research Center (MRC). MRC is the ONLY conservative organization dedicated to exposing the liars on ABC, CBS, NBC, and the rest of the Fake News media.

In fact, our work has successfully gotten CNN staff FIRED from their jobs.

[_**You can help the Media Research Center defeat the Fake News media. I set an URGENT goal of raising $10,000 from Babylon Bee readers. Click here to donate $10, $50, $100, $250, $500, $1,000, or ANY amount today. >>>**_](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z6a386nsqs5m98bikoe96hrqlvskh15ri41godlg_rp22sh2s8i66pr7chimcor1c1h2bs)

**Here are 10 breaking Hunter Biden stories and how long the left-wing media covered them.**

1. Hunter Admits Laptop Was His - **Total Coverage: 1 minute, 45 seconds**
2. Hunter Email on Ukraine Awfully Specific, Raises Questions About His Access to Classified Docs In Biden House- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
3. Hunter Email Offered to Sell Intel on Russian Oligarchs to Alcoa- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
4. Hunter Had a Home Office in Same Delaware House as Classified Documents- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
5. Joe Biden’s Niece Offered $85K Job from Major Biden Donor, She Called it “Below Minimum Wage”- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
6. Joe Biden Named In Email Found on Hunter Laptop In Seeking Multi-Million Dollar Natural Gas Deal with China- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
7. Hunter Allegedly Refused to Pay Female Assistant, Unless She Sent Him Nude Pics- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
8. Hunter Had Access to Joe’s Corvette That Was Garaged Next to Classified Docs- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
9. Former Intel Official Admits He Thought Hunter Laptop Story Was Real But Signed Letter Disavowing It Anyway- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**
10. Hunter’s Art Dealer Stonewalls House GOP Investigation- **Total Coverage:****0 seconds**

Any one of these stories would’ve been widely covered if it was a Republican President’s son, but the left-wing media has been almost completely silent.

I am counting on you to help us reach our $10,000 goal before March to expose the radical left-wing media. Your support empowers MRC to expose liars in the media, spread the truth, and ultimately save Free Speech.

[_**Click here to show your support and help us**_ _**DEFEAT the left-wing media**_ _** >>>**_](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zgve0lh22r06q1cvh6sb4utvht86smhs4kdj7h00_rp22sh2s8i66pr7chimcor1c1h2bs)

_If you and I allow the mainstream media to censor stories as important to America’s security as this, what else will they try to get away with?_

We cannot allow the left-wing media to destroy our Republic, determine our elections, enact socialism, or brainwash our children. That’s why I’m asking for your support.

When the MRC was founded in 1987, over 75% of Americans trusted the left-wing media. Since then, MRC has successfully SHATTERED America’s trust in the left-wing media -- over 90% of Americans state they DO NOT trust the media anymore.

**If you are with the 90% of Americans who DO NOT trust the media, then please send a gift to MRC today!**

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Let's turn Adam Frisch's strong run in '22 into a win in '24--send Boebert packing and take back Kevin McCarhthy's gavel. Will you join us in helping replace a conspiracy-minded fool with a results-oriented, liberal-minded fighter?
body:
Take back McCarthy’s gavel
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tee08769a-b052-4985-841b-43591c8812bb/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

---
| | | | | Peter, **We have a gun crisis, a climate crisis, inequality--both economic and ethnic.**_To quote President Andy Shepherd, played by Michael Douglas in "The American President":_ “We have serious problems to solve, and we need serious people to solve them. And whatever your particular problem is, I promise you Bob Rumson is not the least bit interested in solving it.” _**Change "Bob Rumson" to Lauren Boebert, and the speech is perfect. Adam Frisch, who announced he's challenging Boebert again after losing in the closest House race in the country, is someone we backed early. **__We knew of his talent, and what a fool Boebert is. We knew the state, district makeup, her idiocy and his candidacy created a real chance to win. So, once again, we're 100% __behind him_. [**_Let's turn Frisch's strong run in '22 into a win in '24--send Boebert packing and take back Kevin McCarhthy's gavel. Will you join us in giving Adam Frisch $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to replace a conspiracy-minded fool with a results-oriented, liberal-minded fighter?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6d7a5c2f-7005-4427-a78d-990d7c18c30d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

---
| _**Frisch hasn't disappointed, either. He made his own Andy Shepherd speech on Twitter yesterday, reacting to yet another imbecilic, conspiracy tweet by Boebert** :_ [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Taff5eb18-d8ce-4c35-9882-d2592ca88007/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _To paraphrase Andy Shepherd: **We have serious problems, and Adam Frish is a serious person. And Boebert? She isn't the slightest bit interested in solving your problems** , whatever they may be._ [**_Let's turn Frisch's strong run in '22 into a win in '24--send Boebert packing and take back Kevin McCarhthy's gavel. Will you join us in giving Adam Frisch $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to replace a conspiracy-minded fool with a results-oriented, liberal-minded fighter?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T75af1285-ea02-4ac2-b229-bc3acaadfa58/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Thank you for your dedication. Cliff

---
| | |
---
| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb3f22ccb-9c11-47f0-b5f0-ae478672f5b9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Stephen Miller (America First Legal)
subject: Emergency action to stop illegal immigration
body:
| | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62354928:J9c-G7GZN:m:1:3331421248:6AF2406F4D5A66992DD7AEAB6EE12E66:r)
---
**BREAKING NEWS: "America First Legal & Texas File For An Emergency Injunction To Block Biden's Most Extreme Open Borders Decree..."**
Peter, Did you see the headline above recently? **The situation is dire.** The far-left is cheering as DHS attempts to roll out Biden's most insidious and radical open borders edict to date:**a parole program for illegal aliens**. **This lawless action is a threat to American sovereignty. ** That's why _America First Legal is taking action_ to stop it. We're proud to work with the courageous Ken Paxton to represent Texas and a coalition of twenty states as we seek an **emergency preliminary injunction** to shut down this pernicious program, and prevent it from injecting a new wave of illegal aliens into America. [**Support America First Legal Here >>**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62354928:J9c-G7GZN:m:1:3331421248:6AF2406F4D5A66992DD7AEAB6EE12E66:r) The unlawful program allows illegal aliens to obtain advance approval to enter the United States - despite no basis in the law for them doing so. And the preliminary injunction we just filed for _**will stop this program cold while we fight it in court!**_ Biden's staggeringly RADICAL plan to **mass import illegal aliens** directly to the United States without any legal basis for doing so is nothing more than a brazen attempt to accelerate stratospheric migration levels while concealing its sheer volume by transporting illegal aliens directly into the American interior. If we don't stop Biden's program the damage will be catastrophic: **a deluge of illegal migrants flooding our labor markets, burdening taxpayers, jeopardizing public health and safety, and stretching our safety net to the breaking point. ** [_To win our fight to_** _stop this traitorous campaign_** _to transform our country with mass illegal immigration we need your help. Please support our efforts right now with a generous donation,_**_thank you! >> _**_ _](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62354928:J9c-G7GZN:m:1:3331421248:6AF2406F4D5A66992DD7AEAB6EE12E66:r) _Thank you for your crucial support as we fight this lawless scheme!_ Stephen Miller President, America First Legal

name: Tom Toomey, American Comeback Chairman
subject: Vince Lombardi once said “Winning isn’t everything…”
body:
The NJ GOP is losing - but we don’t have to
| | | | [](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Xe4aLXbM3KllIvIxaU2haWNmPslpF9iKUC4auDuCunZ4WnRhuZ62rdiowfCitb6RcxVUpG5CHuiWVKMJg-eb_LozYEp1nOA1Owl3gr46cxH75n0NWL7x7z2lEjMp84wHY1rwGV5eiDdP7fHpeWtfkmwxc-FQ7yOiPL89aZKA1cKjOXAhyovuJ7Az8cx7gS3bicmo_MZqV2x8n0pKIziCamJSE5AzQCVGfgSszk5OG0QpPPdZdrZ4C5hcEcecc8H4DZ0i5egR1Fk2Gb2uI396TscMWCCdqAeLgABXWxsO8ZWw9DrT5lBxcHE2j_QIFNWdYT-xluoQtXrsxDJaEBXuvtmK4gqLdofM&c=bUKUO3hSoloW2Goqvkj2LLsIcYFv59T4tgkshrU2yUOg35okST4jHw==&ch=0hb0fTkWu-u_iIPERKWjE_IjqOqDwQH2jsJR5NA-kdJJYaWaADRBdA==)
---
| Peter,
In 2021, elections across the great State of New Jersey showed we can turn this state red. But we lost because of ONE reason: mail-in ballots.
The Democrats constantly win because they take advantage of mail-in ballots and their operation is well organized and funded.
Republicans lose because we have NO PLAN to help hard-working and busy Republican voters safely and securely submit their vote through the mail.
The American Comeback PAC plans on changing that.
Our goal is to target New Jersey's hard-working voters that are up at the crack of dawn and get home when the polls are already closed. We NEED to ensure they send in their ballots and have their voices heard because they are the heart and soul of this great state.
The NJ GOP has done nothing to help this crucial group get their vote in -- and it is costing us elections.
If they won't do it, then the American Comeback PAC will and help ensure your freedoms are returned to you in this state.
New Jersey's own Vince Lombardi once said "Winning isn't everything, it is the only thing". Well, we need to win now more than ever.
We're tired of losing, and if you are too, we hope you'll join our efforts to turn New Jersey into a RED STATE in 2023 and beyond!
