name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: SIGN: Demand Pete Buttigieg reinstate safety measures the railroads killed
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Three weeks ago, a train carrying toxic chemicals derailed in Ohio,
causing a massive explosion and mass evacuations. But it could have been
prevented. According to The Lever’s reporting, in the decades leading up
to the disaster, corporate lobbyists successfully weakened safety rules
for trains carrying hazardous materials — just like this one.

Get this: this train spilled chemicals so toxic they were burned to
prevent an explosion. But regulations were gutted to the point that it
wasn’t even classified as a train carrying hazardous flammable materials
or required to use new braking systems designed to prevent derailments.

First: Chemical lobbyists got Obama officials to exempt trains like the
one in Ohio from hazmat safety rules and rail lobbyists got Trump
officials to repeal rules requiring better brakes on trains.

Then: The Railroad workers union warned that these safety issues were a
“ticking time bomb.”

Now: The people of East Palestine, OH — and those that depend on the Ohio
River — will pay the price for years to come.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/128168?redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjdem-20230223%3Fsource%3Dem20230223-27683&t=1001&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk ]Will you sign our petition demanding that Pete Buttigieg take immediate
measures to regulate train safety — to protect our rail workers and
communities?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/128168?redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjdem-20230223%3Fsource%3Dem20230223-27683&t=1002&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk ]Sign the Petition

The Biden administration can and must do something now. Pete Buttigieg can
stand up to corporate lobbyists and reinstate the regulations that will
better protect us — and workers — from trains carrying toxic chemicals.

Until now, Pete Buttigieg has done little to expand train safety rules. In
fact, his department is considering a rule right now that was promoted by
railroad corporations to weaken train safety.

This is completely unacceptable, especially as a preventable environmental
and public health disaster unfolds in Ohio. Trains carrying toxic,
flammable material are passing through communities every day — and
lobbyists have succeeded in exempting them from tough safety laws. No
more.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/128168?redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjdem-20230223%3Fsource%3Dem20230223-27683&t=1003&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk ]We are demanding that Secretary Buttigieg immediately use his existing
power to make safety rules much stronger, and put our people and workers
over railroad profits. Will you sign on now?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/128168?redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjdem-20230223%3Fsource%3Dem20230223-27683&t=1004&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk ]Add Your Name

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1005&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1006&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230223-27683-footer&t=1007&refcode2=27683_1203867_6HGrPk&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1008&akid=27683%2E1203867%2E6HGrPk

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

clean_body: | | | Peter, It’s Julia from Team Josh Hawley. I’ve spent my career working in conservative politics, and after a while, you start to see some patterns. Every election year, there are one or two races that stand out from the rest: these are the closest races with the most riding on them. | | Peter, last election, Josh only won with 51.4% of the vote. And this year, it could be even tighter. If we’re going to keep this conservative fighter in the Senate in 2024, we have to start NOW, and that’s why I’m reaching out to you. We can’t do this without you, Peter. America is counting on you. Thank you, Julia Team Hawley | | Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee | |

Team — Jason has always gone where the fight is. After 9/11, Jason went active duty and became a Captain with the Army Rangers. name: Pramila's iPhone
subject: [1 missed message from Pramila Jayapal]
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
#
| | | | | | | | | | |
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---
| Hi Peter, it’s Pramila here! Do you have a moment? Pramila
| I wanted to thank you for being a part of our people-powered movement. For over six years, supporters like you have powered this campaign and kept our work going through thick and thin. Pramila
| And look where it got us -- this movement has won unprecedented victories in the last two years alone.

Our Progressive Caucus played a critical role in securing the first gun control legislation in decades, bold climate action, and historic investments in infrastructure. We made student debt forgiveness a top priority for the Biden administration and passed marriage equality. Pramila
| I’m proud of what we’ve done together in a few short years. And I’m also proud to say that my campaign is sustained by donations from our grassroots base -- not corporate PACs or special interests. Hundreds of thousands of people have chipped in over the years, and right now our average donation is just $16.16! Pramila
| Let me be clear: none of this would be possible without you, Peter. I need you by my side as we continue our fight for the next two years. [If you’re ready to keep fighting with me, will you chip in $3? Every dollar adds up in the long run.](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/37924?refcode=em20230223-2203&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=2203%2E420207%2ED-yt12)

Thank you for your steadfast support. Until next time! Pramila
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/37924?refcode=em20230223-2203&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=2203%2E420207%2ED-yt12)**

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20230223-2203-footer&t=3&refcode2=2203_420207_D-yt12&akid=2203%2E420207%2ED-yt12)**

Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=6505%2E878179%2E6TJiwx). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=6505%2E878179%2E6TJiwx).**

body:
It’s time for the WFP February Newsletter, your monthly update on how we’re fighting to build a country where everyone can thrive. | | | **It’s time for the WFP February Newsletter,** your monthly update on how we’re fighting to build a country where everyone can thrive. Keep reading for more info on how WFP champions and activists have been making change in our cities, our states, and in Congress this month. **Let’s get started:**
* * *
_**We share our vision in the WFP State of the Union Response**_ **Earlier this month, U.S. Rep. Delia Ramirez (IL-03) delivered the sixth annual Working Families Party response to President Biden’s State of the Union Address.** [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDMdk0Xc5F3XT7oWSkA6eoLmCcYcyAipDPLbzQeBpFBzg1674U3iS2P642V4K7vAnf0D3QVySbKfr4BRrmCfFWmpd9zWBacf_lpuoCFA_tkjuKkuf3KM-kXWS7QpyD2UWXQFOfijPHSkrQWPLSw6Ig-J1dghHKvj0FFqzvp101CTr0-oJuYUZRfOaPIbz11wTBKsZl-0ymhaCysJubQQPcrrNElZbXcTGI4Xj5obIfbFh10i8mqYR_Y9vejbXs6B_si7qXxyXjC0Rpx5ZvKH8Vn8/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h0/qSRk26mFNQCp4_A6qX5tdDo4Kd-MURo9UKX9RfTjNcM) If you missed it, **[check out some highlights](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDE-osrDsyUs4KS0eRiq-k7SUEv4-aVenR6vLTv4hSaUWOzpAFDGBxRvG4MMGn3bFR8WHdxI25La3J42f3ablWzIEnww7x6WTAYZ-kOX3tkq_MrC2ozfsekZi0ClntWMEqppuuIlPl-euZl17wsqy-NZOcUQ5AZN3DTZf1uVoEsQujaLhP_AOKGHYRszCN7EweG9n7PK33UxmUbYl3IJuSwTOBEoKobV_S7N5k1qGhUcUivm-mcZ8Fj0srmDGt3D_ZyX1iW_SAVLLbxi_ASPeEF8/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h1/Ir-2ZzbJ1I0wi7mci4h79rGNnDudDDhfiAlKHaYJEaI)** or **[watch the full recording in English, Spanish, or ASL here.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDMdk0Xc5F3XT7oWSkA6eoLmCcYcyAipDPLbzQeBpFBzgMugxndVpOqtvpprnAzVHb7Qa1DY2OtnF4ZNkrmLIEoxFQ-NNsPGUFZSHT1zg3ZsC9lDEJsfXBzkyuiNtL8um4RdNmnX7Rf-SZs289BtXEfQ3aZ8YyiYBysK-LfsQ7U2962tQJvB6mKf6Hj2I2zcLwHNGD98EtTm4w9pPzWDOw-14razOgZppVTaSVoCDDU6hK3ypMLW_xo82Fmw2NA8Z7bG8ZlJQJ_KEly-ym_x1XCE/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h2/oMB_62JskcH62FJAR1L88X5kqkLzV2yL6kaMX6-O2j8)** We were proud to share our vision for a government that has working people’s backs in a speech that **[James Downie of MSNBC](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDIj1-3iymdRNaOHWQ7RCITFz0OzvfC-FTJ5nBPiT53eLjRtTD5wADz44izTToOGsh6fy9dyRqJrpI1SvU_pISy9OPHL7q5WjUXPNoJQG2ssovjpa7-H3YrBrfw_6YQgpqw11wQteKkxU1xy2QIj7tjkp4JNvahIfVmtiXe1vUvfYt2nIs8VhOPgehf8UAgjJbqCbc4QC620l3QiPl3I4mjtw9J03tStJgc2O9HDWLhH2ysFXdxfPfI0Vt8EiZoh9r7FLRJdBG8hGuU4cjPvmTbzBPKak16vHBe3OAL4_JIyJd7B-K2tWlZpsJLS8Ut_8GucpKK-zB5d-bpy6e34PN5vK65OY9L7S5AWhaAObq9wf/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h3/KEqv24SNHyLbpnhXnw792EYerv4ZCa20BUCVnpxKi7E)** called “real leadership.” You can read more coverage of the WFP State of Union Response in **[Politico,](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDL0195r-gNaKL0NXw0E6jsauSrJkPNMUkcg29HkqhlLtA00ryfAgLyuBJRpa2AlOe1a_s_OI5yEEDYFcuATS4H0zY6DKB6vLirzuYr3u4NzMcvK4Aukkze4YPImX0uRpnNWeU73qNWTBqLsOEHsRArUbpuhHn1nMEjettOw0kxBV4XQXjBpCR79JChKuWqvCKhN-5zY24D86Bc99zwIDs7DhHuzWatmhxs-EWwCBo-lkkJDlAkgUKawypRQLx1PXJS7ekXjfM2dK-VyyhKT5NbBXzYTFOd-YkMoTBk-ezJM1d5FT-mu3i_98DfxAHeD4to6xxERBosYNSC6aRfALY9KG_RiQsC83LD6bLLb3rbZWhwpmyahEoX08-dpAVDC41A/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h4/WDhEGyLGHIxaBrxE-HMOtlZsBjK8BcqAve8Kjl3qHyk) [Rolling Stone,](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDCdhR8VxIAY_jvJoRv5mBT7wuklWDY_2XbGHgeG5ckGbUaD-1C3PmjSpxcNaPkk2wxpBSzKVABE5f0GbPCeYSs5rFO_c0fGERr5FvPcyftiJgvRH71_7BksSeVAm439KEj7o3s5crIJAE3k2MYGVctQLlpIljexdxDF9a6dHBkQH1H41goIwyXe0bqlQBYw2qkFZEU6ELxPhku1HHgtO7KubO6Utmo8DUBY2jHQ70aNBq7waqqkONGlivSfLVJQuNQH1Oye1BniXboggsMjs_WzlkyYCG63G4_8sHJD-O-XvNHvqMt1VouELliLwhhC26idXAU2eOOTqTHv4pFWAe4uppMUCJweUxNzbnl5Mz5BnOP_aJkd-ymGH4BffLMy7Jg/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h5/9yLgxt0KLiAvg9tZwKHau4LA_oCLw4jvA_jdx2RaE9c)** and **[MSNBC.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDIj1-3iymdRNaOHWQ7RCITFz0OzvfC-FTJ5nBPiT53eLjRtTD5wADz44izTToOGsh6fy9dyRqJrpI1SvU_pISy9OPHL7q5WjUXPNoJQG2ssovjpa7-H3YrBrfw_6YQgpqyi9UIhKUQn_5oTGzjhSd8HUOv9k9LgNzq_Ux0URBwOa-JYQJYlt06tQ_YUSm70k1FpzvWbPSjs5IXGrJi-6cGHTic8aazhPKH7wmdgoMER-Trk8uWRH8ilDOgfOb3cB8Atathi-ZDx3-GjjZ9Tfvq6zSV3-JBc-0Q21pOofKoXruWl4txstzu3ClMpUiI9r05EAGpaMkw49_vJNGsYnMDF99e5OD9e0e97blW96q5sb/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h6/rlsHifi9VbushtFPViGdA_pC4ki1Aa77z3CxGu9HMEk)**
* * *
**_WFP champions are making waves in Congress_ ** In January, WFP champions **Greg Casar, Delia Ramirez, Summer Lee, Maxwell Frost, Jasmine Crockett, and Becca Balint** were sworn into Congress — and they’re already making waves as they fight for worker’s rights, climate change, immigration reform, and more. **[Pennsylvania Rep. Summer Lee shared with Politico how she plans to wield power,](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDL0195r-gNaKL0NXw0E6jsY9h91fLUEFD5NJku5jouZInZidQPU1UvTZexW-GvUqC8RvlrfX1ndaD1fOkWpndRPwxJ-0S1LHFx_2TxP6uCsEHZezIEE1JAmRnmImRcwg-t5BYcSvM_Q5CvUEnIqy8nL4orrBJV9lWF5-x6m2seykcTEYN75awZuWLgs12bYJg_h330rFj0dUmHIBCTOSp3Mcr_vCORExf6CXhIrBi0f5ezAd54tUCbv41zg8yEURfPC81PkmFC39k-BCPt6aVYS5ygver7tOOqqknl2e8A8xDqDJxgN6zg8BED_yaMvAwCs0eg721IDMzlKQh_D2DacL3jRfhKIjO3lcbW-rDJDW/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h7/rAe2k66ThVET2xWAPK-VT_gBPwYlVxt_cQ3TWJeh6c0)** “I think that as legislators, our job is to agenda-set. It’s to govern, it’s to create policy, but it’s also to put forth that best case, and bring people over to us. That’s what progressives have to do, whether we’re in the majority or the minority...That’s going to be where a lot of power is: in expanding the realm of what’s possible.” **[Read more.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDL0195r-gNaKL0NXw0E6jsY9h91fLUEFD5NJku5jouZInZidQPU1UvTZexW-GvUqC8RvlrfX1ndaD1fOkWpndRPwxJ-0S1LHFx_2TxP6uCsEHZezIEE1JAmRnmImRcwg-t5BYcSvM_Q5CvUEnIqy8nJrGiNiAcapo-sDqP86u8ElB1vwQZDFlBwDohTRdJOJg5B49Rx0Q9vdZAWMLWNMlPjKLClFlQShW0nV27etRoAq48s9MscItNZeCYALdQ4Vf3nHp43c-uqNow4X_9Uk6FryUBXVhH6NfcYZ1B19dmYH_U3vMbee7Z293Cv8D0SFs7OjlT4ZMsXvvABNwb3c9QfxRELFbBKGpzhXR4WMv3JH/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h8/DTFOofRewxMru3sCbQmz2AkjyM3qdvcsie6tfeSMImI)**
* * *
**_WFP makes an impact in Virginia, Wisconsin races_ ** The midterms may be over, but election season continues with key races around the country in 2023. **On Tuesday, voters in Virginia’s 4th Congressional District elected Jennifer McClellan, who will be the first Black congresswoman from Virginia.** **We were proud to support Jennifer in her competitive primary, and again in the general election against a Republican opponent who was at the January 6 insurrection.** She’s a bold leader who passed landmark laws to protect voting rights, safeguard abortion access, tackle climate change, rebuild crumbling schools, expand Obamacare in Virginia, protect workers rights, and reform Virginia’s criminal justice system as a State Senator. We know Jennifer will keep up the fight as she joins other WFP champions in Congress. [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/dSCQfi9FLISmU3ZE3bfPhkjyHYqt8H8hDV-_Pd8DlhN4z-pz9keHd1CtmEen-uqiGRu9REj7n_hPw51lmKP16Aew-AcXTbvo_ZS-1kCWSs13S4LTXPbUSzoHs7dIttCWGRf5oq7YLd1a6ox0JvH0OruyWMNadc4csfvn7EbwevWz811-Yum1xmdiOlr0SQkfBWCTA8sZOJI5XDhqwc2oS4kpwb48IJmFZfxJaPK-7MFJmMEFw9eQ0118wkNO_r5gaHspIGqQ0ZSdNq_pPvOl6RqvqAtjWwJpZ8dVccmrXtvkgq1ZmHiuu52dib5oTW8NwWnnU1rM6wyW2XhoE3dPNg/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h9/ZiVvlzhpufZ00xgoautPjsVFOf1fm3UhWCjWccnyQbM) **In Wisconsin, Working Families Power has been turning out voters for an open swing seat on the Wisconsin Supreme Court** — what may be the most important race of the year. Here’s what **Wisconsin Working Families Party State Director Jake Spence** had to say about Tuesday’s primary results: “Tonight’s results give us a fighting chance. We’re 42 days away from striking down Wisconsin’s abortion ban and protecting our democracy from a right-wing takeover. Wisconsinites know how to fight like hell for what we believe in, and with our basic freedoms on the ballot, anti-choice candidates can count on us to spend the next 6 weeks organizing to keep them as far from this seat as possible.”
* * *
**_ENDORSEMENT ALERT: Chicago, Philadelphia, New York, and more_ ** And we’ve got more elections coming down the pike, starting with the **Chicago mayoral election** on February 28. The race could very well go to a runoff — and **[a new poll released this week shows WFP-endorsed Brandon Johnson in second place,](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDGKDXR7k6d3dm2_Ec4oybbatQB553BIz2ZoVvnODJjmS1w_V1ZNlobeI5iK2ZMIb9CgFLH_60SRudv27IwhsYNdeffcZP5ETXurSFyS4QiuCCxJnYxCesczfNwvxs0DPVEl_x3Q-xJ8KZWgdsHm1cGN6MrfGm3RlE597YnrtOD_06-3ugMz4rxdCgbDq20CE3ZjQZDjQCijq61hmIyv6gGAvcxLfZzC54fANmBVt3lRLyxsNdr7Tjg6zTMa5jxTbr20mcEKG4xnjZwXXehEYDYcAShky0lCsRT0mYrAkO5yu1Io2YtAoasv5GPPCtKzKkgdcx-u0A-YKEzsFafWGzcA/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h10/8JFwv-L_6uiGYHrGhGaJRQGwEpDvqpERHiv4O2RGZyQ)** behind the Fraternal Order of Police’s favorite candidate. **As Mayor of Chicago, Brandon would work to ensure fully funded public schools, affordable housing, green jobs, and access to mental health care.** The Pennsylvania Working Families Party is throwing down hard, as we organize for **Helen Gym for Philadelphia Mayor, as well as to elect three candidates on the WFP ballot line — Jarrett Smith for City Commissioner and Nicolas O’Rourke and Kendra Brooks for Philadelphia City Council.** If we win, we’ll shut out Republicans from city government. And with the help of supporters like you, our Philadelphia slate has already raised $340,000 to take on the Republican Party in the city. **[Read more.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDMdk0Xc5F3XT7oWSkA6eoLnNBhme4o1pf6TXVzRYlrKDCipzYXXvmt80vA6mnoQfdpuy4DYfL8yfz7QWzan4m5PMWAGSxLmbridQznzc87Qu-UuNm2JdzUiqoVRPk61j9A5CFFmERAErGN2b5EnMx-ntEG1Pdwq2J3uxAZwXtHXQXGy4lczqj7X_AKZoLPXw-66HBC_UgMHg2Xdm9FdrDs8o8WkMLjvsgdLneAPQIvcpwT9Mbuv_ze-onwyvr0tk7nkLxYHXvb2XmV0niVwkQvjMcThViBldBzcNXT7NRZjmf3HihnDcW_KQ2-k3NTf66MyrVU-t1XzCqif8keC4ruY/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h11/Up2IdtZN4-bw2-JJFB4mf1HFcdcJ6fELm5asa3Dsq_w)** **In New York City, we released 16 new endorsements for City Council** after successfully expanding abortion access, increasing services to survivors of domestic violence, improving access to Narcan in bars and clubs, mitigating the rat crisis, and more in the last two years. **[New York WFP Director Sochie Nnaemeka shares:](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDMdk0Xc5F3XT7oWSkA6eoLmpfwNZHR-lLr6rvILBzbx7RFf5JY0xOfNVQl4NAlNgcR44s4WtzhvBaoK9MsGEButqPlaUk7JX9HcQzydVnxh1IcuQmUHSPtjnMOVRdI1JvABGO8JTXZv-QWMWm7pUoVyQlcEI40h7kUIL9CaHNC00GrUuo9RHzl0jK2N8Q3SK4e5QrSMxAar348mKCq8aAal9cPa8_GtxQzAlzDJqeElBWcy-GnJdqTOHlmfFX_7c0Ra22UuLupR9Aq5zZB3H1IQ9k8j9d41WsagZmULG3T-lBQKk4Xb1-ahChYSxbPluLA/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h12/Dvnz1IeoDvrGw3o-0JovyfwEGJBGVdEcEl732u8weXc)** “With a Mayor intent on cutting down public services and critical agencies, we need a strong New York City Council ready to push the kind of policies that will make life better and more affordable for working families. To create a safe and thriving New York, our communities need deep investments, not deep cuts.” [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDMdk0Xc5F3XT7oWSkA6eoLmpfwNZHR-lLr6rvILBzbx7RFf5JY0xOfNVQl4NAlNgcR44s4WtzhvBaoK9MsGEBuvXW3GFVPJn43grlOD2D-I2XTlxD1t0Yc7shq--Ts9byVmbAReQYfHq2T7ZVftmU_BoMOVChxsU9E_CPRJlYerhK-Vde7Q-wwpNYm3aD4sOZ0igqx4ZDjdKWI-4Q1amAHFtHhgYaLwM933tjdzIdDdZqd1KGfeKXWcKykHzcqY6JR-_0gs9mfp0JlLPvLSL3YyMXcBrxOGQxpa0YunbkAeTczZjPttPLLcrDK7Khq3tow/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h13/knswx6yWv2p-93ju3JpDhP80RUEXhO5NuR04_5F9qEU) **[In Ithaca, the NYWFP shared its endorsement](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDLveIHoqaeE8oK57Prq16cHtNu8ssjlX3xdtL_Ql03E7WnBp3k8MQnsJVdhofiW8TScK040XYBlJnEVIXxOAwqG2X1t2h6eFJNHzXSlCUYttKQfmQE4hM3v0Z1e3GnF1_6Esw7CjKfWSb6T1SoudOHmF0WSVzTJbIPawnbQJrOBHw5bDJMMnEuZopPknm9L6TM43mWsTSULzACchnuvvDM_naHiZ5BjugW306IJoQE7gePmFkvlpe6iiUJKbQIHZbFSo8TdVx_ONhiVFUaRIn-Fa8nq-oVa0sfFrsKwuOihP-MxDR9_G5eZ12ZaUni6ekG_qUXhYMsUqEgfsC0Vd0lI0-YckU7fG_xWfC1TRiov7GEgnuxQ8TZsPxWJhwNonpsy43lEr9h8WA_y_Dh7Q3P4/3ty/vMMbRUpmQ6qGKSEEhMOM5Q/h14/hR05C9qPVPQ71JBmsQfBmu7Pngvo7xiW2TjGkt1Mvbg)** of Robert Cantelmo for Mayor, as well as candidates for Town Justice and five City Council seats. **_Spotlight on WFP member Apurva Tandon_ ** “I’ve lived in Pennsylvania for almost six years. I grew up in decidedly blue states, and moving to PA for grad school was an impetus to pay more attention to electoral politics and eventually start volunteering, originally with Bernie’s 2020 campaign. **I now find it incredibly empowering to be part of WFP’s volunteer network.** Through WFP I’ve learned or practiced lots of organizing skills (like phone/text banking or facilitating volunteer training virtually). I’ve had opportunities to support recruitment and party building through writing and speaking. I’ve learned about and joined endorsement interviews for local office. I’ve met and become part of a truly engaged, responsive community. But beyond so many personal learning opportunities, it’s empowering to give my time to a political movement that I feel genuinely aligned with — rather than one I feel forced to align with under a two-party structure designed against the interests of most people.” **— Apurva Tandon**

clean_body: It’s time for the WFP February Newsletter, your monthly update on how we’re fighting to build a country where everyone can thrive. | | | It’s time for the WFP February Newsletter, your monthly update on how we’re fighting to build a country where everyone can thrive. Keep reading for more info on how WFP champions and activists have been making change in our cities, our states, and in Congress this month. Let’s get started: We share our vision in the WFP State of the Union Response Earlier this month, U.S. Rep. Delia Ramirez (IL-03) delivered the sixth annual Working Families Party response to President Biden’s State of the Union Address. If you missed it, or We were proud to share our vision for a government that has working people’s backs in a speech that called “real leadership.” You can read more coverage of the WFP State of Union Response in and WFP champions are making waves in Congress In January, WFP champions Greg Casar, Delia Ramirez, Summer Lee, Maxwell Frost, Jasmine Crockett, and Becca Balint were sworn into Congress — and they’re already making waves as they fight for worker’s rights, climate change, immigration reform, and more. “I think that as legislators, our job is to agenda-set. It’s to govern, it’s to create policy, but it’s also to put forth that best case, and bring people over to us. That’s what progressives have to do, whether we’re in the majority or the minority...That’s going to be where a lot of power is: in expanding the realm of what’s possible.” WFP makes an impact in Virginia, Wisconsin races The midterms may be over, but election season continues with key races around the country in 2023. On Tuesday, voters in Virginia’s 4th Congressional District elected Jennifer McClellan, who will be the first Black congresswoman from Virginia. We were proud to support Jennifer in her competitive primary, and again in the general election against a Republican opponent who was at the January 6 insurrection. She’s a bold leader who passed landmark laws to protect voting rights, safeguard abortion access, tackle climate change, rebuild crumbling schools, expand Obamacare in Virginia, protect workers rights, and reform Virginia’s criminal justice system as a State Senator. We know Jennifer will keep up the fight as she joins other WFP champions in Congress. In Wisconsin, Working Families Power has been turning out voters for an open swing seat on the Wisconsin Supreme Court — what may be the most important race of the year. Here’s what Wisconsin Working Families Party State Director Jake Spence had to say about Tuesday’s primary results: “Tonight’s results give us a fighting chance. We’re 42 days away from striking down Wisconsin’s abortion ban and protecting our democracy from a right-wing takeover. Wisconsinites know how to fight like hell for what we believe in, and with our basic freedoms on the ballot, anti-choice candidates can count on us to spend the next 6 weeks organizing to keep them as far from this seat as possible.” ENDORSEMENT ALERT: Chicago, Philadelphia, New York, and more And we’ve got more elections coming down the pike, starting with the Chicago mayoral election on February 28. The race could very well go to a runoff — and behind the Fraternal Order of Police’s favorite candidate. As Mayor of Chicago, Brandon would work to ensure fully funded public schools, affordable housing, green jobs, and access to mental health care. The Pennsylvania Working Families Party is throwing down hard, as we organize for Helen Gym for Philadelphia Mayor, as well as to elect three candidates on the WFP ballot line — Jarrett Smith for City Commissioner and Nicolas O’Rourke and Kendra Brooks for Philadelphia City Council. If we win, we’ll shut out Republicans from city government. And with the help of supporters like you, our Philadelphia slate has already raised $340,000 to take on the Republican Party in the city. In New York City, we released 16 new endorsements for City Council after successfully expanding abortion access, increasing services to survivors of domestic violence, improving access to Narcan in bars and clubs, mitigating the rat crisis, and more in the last two years. “With a Mayor intent on cutting down public services and critical agencies, we need a strong New York City Council ready to push the kind of policies that will make life better and more affordable for working families. To create a safe and thriving New York, our communities need deep investments, not deep cuts.” of Robert Cantelmo for Mayor, as well as candidates for Town Justice and five City Council seats. These champions will work hard to create a safer, healthier, and more equitable community for all. And in Josh Michtom, Tiana Hercules, and Alex Thomas understand that working people need allies who are unapologetically on their side in local government and have pledged to prioritize infrastructure, education, health, and housing. — a former Special Education teacher for nearly three decades and a progressive voice on the Jonesboro City Council. Donya went through a WFP candidate training program, and is fighting for affordable housing, expanded wifi, the protection of green spaces, and more sidewalks and bus lanes for her city. Do you know someone amazing running for office? You can find a full list of our endorsed candidates Spotlight on WFP member Apurva Tandon “I’ve lived in Pennsylvania for almost six years. I grew up in decidedly blue states, and moving to PA for grad school was an impetus to pay more attention to electoral politics and eventually start volunteering, originally with Bernie’s 2020 campaign. I now find it incredibly empowering to be part of WFP’s volunteer network. Through WFP I’ve learned or practiced lots of organizing skills (like phone/text banking or facilitating volunteer training virtually). I’ve had opportunities to support recruitment and party building through writing and speaking. I’ve learned about and joined endorsement interviews for local office. I’ve met and become part of a truly engaged, responsive community. But beyond so many personal learning opportunities, it’s empowering to give my time to a political movement that I feel genuinely aligned with — rather than one I feel forced to align with under a two-party structure designed against the interests of most people.” — Apurva Tandon Upcoming Events Working Families Power Wisconsin Supreme Court Postcard Parties Working Families Power is spreading the word about the Wisconsin Supreme Court election this April! Campaign Camp The free, virtual WFP Campaign Camp is designed to provide grassroots organizers and activists with the tools they need to win elections and continue fighting for the poor, the working class, and the marginalized in moments like this. to learn what makes a good candidate, the role of a candidate in a campaign, as well as how to find and recruit good candidates where we need them and prepare them to run in the future. We will also contextualize WFP as a political party, debunk some myths about winning an election, and map out how to get a campaign started. WFP National Director Maurice Mitchell in Brooklyn for a wide-ranging discussion about our movements, our democracy, and where we go from here. WFP Welcome Gathering Whether you’re a new supporter or a longtime WFP member, Welcome Gatherings are an energetic space where you can meet other working people with the same vision of building a world where we can thrive, and not just survive. Thank you for reading all the way through! We know that was a lot of information, and it’s still only a small portion of what WFP champions, staff, activists, and supporters are achieving every month to build a country where everyone can thrive. But this work takes resources, and we need your support. Thank you, and we’ll see you next month! All of us at the Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. ** Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via .
